title: Public opinion trajectory regarding COVID-19 vaccination acceptance in the United States during the 2021 vaccine rollout

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Interpolated based on KFF Vaccine Monitor Feb 2021 trends

events.description: President Joe Biden was inaugurated and established a federal goal of administering 100 million vaccine doses within his first 100 days in office.
The FDA granted Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for the Johnson & Johnson (Janssen) single-dose COVID-19 vaccine, expanding access options.
Public health data indicated a significant decline in vaccine hesitancy across most demographic groups, particularly among young adults and Black Americans.

agents.name: CDC
agents.description: Federal public health agency promoting vaccination

agents.name: White House
agents.description: Executive branch driving federal vaccine distribution

agents.name: Vaccine Skeptic Groups
agents.description: Organized groups opposing mandates and expressing safety concerns

agents.name: General Public
agents.description: The aggregate population of US adults

notes: Pro-side is defined as individuals who have received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine or are willing to get vaccinated as soon as possible. Against-side is defined as individuals who refuse vaccination or are in the 'wait-and-see' category. Data quality is high due to consistent methodology from KFF Vaccine Monitor.
